findings.description: Overall form layout on page 1 is not preserved; the original multi-zone tax form is converted into a largely linear document with key fields reorganized.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has a dense form layout with top header blocks, right-side checkbox panel, inline entry fields, and a two-column-style line/amount presentation. HTML page 1 presents these as a single flowing page with stacked text and later tabular rows, changing the visual grouping and whitespace structure substantially.

findings.description: Barcode/graphic element is not preserved in appearance on page 1.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 contains a prominent vertical barcode near the center of Part 1. HTML page 1 shows only a small dark barcode-like mark, losing the original size, placement, and legibility.

findings.description: Page 2 apportionment worksheet layout is simplified and the original boxed two-panel worksheet structure is not preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows a structured worksheet with two large side-by-side column groups ('Within North Carolina' and 'Total Everywhere') inside a bordered form. HTML page 2 uses a plain table and linear page flow, losing the original boxed worksheet arrangement and spacing cues.

findings.description: Page 3 partner-entry form layout is not preserved; side-by-side partner panels are transformed into a standard table.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 has three distinct vertical partner boxes (Partner 1, Partner 2, Partner 3) with grouped sections A/B/C. HTML page 3 converts this into a flat table with partner columns, removing the boxed form-panel structure and visual grouping.

findings.description: Section hierarchy and emphasis on page 3 are reduced compared with the original form.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 uses strong boxed headers and section labels A, B, and C with tight form-style emphasis. HTML page 3 retains the text but with less visual distinction and weaker separation between instructional text and entry regions.

findings.description: Page 4 form layout and signature area are reorganized into linear sections and tables, losing the original integrated form structure.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 presents Part 4, the tax rate schedule, and the certification/signature block as coordinated boxed form regions with right-aligned amount fields. HTML page 4 renders these as stacked tables and plain text fields, substantially changing the page structure and whitespace grouping.

findings.description: Part 2 table structure is not faithfully preserved; the PDF uses grouped headers and separate factor/result boxes, while the HTML collapses these into a simplified 5-column table and places labels like 'Factor' and '%' as cell contents.
findings.evidence: PDF Page 2 shows grouped headers '1. Within North Carolina' and '2. Total Everywhere' with subcolumns '(a) Beginning Period' and '(b) Ending Period', plus separate right-side factor/result boxes for Lines 7-15. HTML represents this as a single table with columns 'Line Item | 1. Within North Carolina - (a) Beginning Period | ... | 2. Total Everywhere - (b) Ending Period' and inserts 'Factor' or '%' into the final cell of several rows.

findings.description: Part 3 form layout is materially altered from the PDF; section markers A/B/C and partner-entry boxes are flattened into a simple table, which loses original row grouping and form-field structure.
findings.evidence: PDF Page 3 presents 'Part 3' with boxed sections A, B, and C along the left margin and separate form boxes for Partner 1, Partner 2, and Partner 3. HTML converts this to one standard table headed 'Item Description | Partner 1 | Partner 2 | Partner 3' and encodes section markers as text such as '[A] 1. Identifying Number' and '[B] 9. Guaranteed payments...'.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is broken: the document uses multiple h4 headings directly under h3 without a consistent structural representation of page/part hierarchy, and page headers are embedded as generic div text rather than headings.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses '

2012 Partnership Income Tax Return

', then '

North Carolina Department of Revenue

', then multiple '

' for 'Part 1', 'Part 2', 'Part 3', 'Part 4', followed by '

Additions to Income

', '

Deductions from Income

', and '

Tax Rate Schedule

'. The PDF's page-level labels and part structure are visually distinct and not cleanly mapped to heading levels.

findings.description: Some text was altered or normalized in ways that change exact form wording and notation, including checkbox/circle semantics and punctuation/wording in instructions.
findings.evidence: PDF uses explicit selectable circles in the right panel such as 'Initial Return', 'Amended Return', etc., and negative-number circles beside lines; HTML renders these as plain list items and inline symbols like '◯'. PDF wording includes 'Entity is Partnership', 'Entity is LLC', 'Entity has Nonresidenat Owners' and line formatting tied to form controls, while HTML converts these to prose/list format and rewrites some instructions, e.g., 'If amount on Line 1, 3, 5, 7, 8, or 9 is negative, fill in circle. Example: ⚫'.
